title: Are We At An Inflection Point For Capesize Rates
section_path: Are We At An Inflection Point For Capesize Rates
section_path_text: Are We At An Inflection Point For Capesize Rates
summary:
Main
Despite being a year for the records in terms of social impact and economic disruption due to the COVID-19 pandemic, 2020 has proven to be a year of a lot of similarities to 2019 if one looks strictly at dry bulk freight rates. The year begun with significant weakness due to the virus outbreak, which sunk Capesize rates towards zero, very similar to last year, when the rapture of the Brumadinho dam had a similar effect on the freight market. Then, a strong late summer rally drove Capesize rates towards the mid-30,000 level, followed by a sharp correction down to the mid-20,000, though admittedly the correction this time around has been deeper than last year's more controlled drop.
